evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Establish and maintain strong metadata, documentation, lineage, and semantic consistency for analytics datasets and reporting layers
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Rigour signals (metadata, documentation, lineage, governance, semantic consistency) dominate with no velocity/speed language present.
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: eCommerce/retail BI reporting affects business decisions but carries no financial reporting, regulatory, or compliance language.
evidence.data_team_maturity: Collaborate with Data Engineering on sustainable data pipelines
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Named BI and Data Engineering teams and established KPI/metric governance practices indicate a mid-stage, coordinating data function.
evidence.jd_authorship: Good understanding of data warehousing concepts, dimensional modeling, and transformation workflows
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Specific technical terms (dimensional modeling, Tableau/Power BI, metadata/lineage) give a concrete picture of daily analytics engineering work.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Develop and maintain curated data models, reusable metrics, and governed datasets that support management reporting, self-service analytics, and insight generation
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The role centres on serving BI/reporting layers and self-service analytics for internal consumers across the organization, the internal_data pattern.
evidence.autonomy_level: Define transformation logic and business rules for consistency
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Defining transformation logic and establishing metadata standards is ownership-leaning, but much of the role is framed as working with stakeholders to understand requirements, giving mixed autonomy.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: No AI tool or AI infrastructure mention appears anywhere in the extracted JD text.
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: No explicit testing or data quality framework language appears; governance and lineage are mentioned but not framed as a testing practice.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: Support KPI setup and metric governance
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Metric governance and semantic consistency are a stated concern, reflecting operational reliability rather than compliance or regulatory risk.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Design and maintain analytics-ready datasets, transformation logic, and semantic models
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: The role mixes designing new semantic models and datasets with maintaining existing curated data models and pipelines.
evidence.language_gate: Proficient English; Chinese is a plus
evidence.loss_aversion: Support KPI setup and metric governance — reflects concern for metric/data consistency rather than compliance or regulatory risk.
